preamble: The palatalization of the bilabial nasal [m] into the palatal nasal [ɲ] is a rare phonetic process that typically involves intermediate stages where the labial closure is lost in favor of a palatal one, often triggered by following high front vowels or glides.

examples.note: Common in diminutive and passive formation (labial palatalization).

sources: Doke, C. M. (1926). The Phonetics of the Zulu Language.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
